extraction.one_line_summary: PHILIPSEN Jasper won the sprint after TARAXAY Rein crashed ~11km from finish, handing ALISSON Kenny the race lead via misfortune.
tactical_patterns: UAE led out MOLANO Juan Sebastian until 1.6km but burned matches too early, allowing Deceuninck perfect drafting position.
Deceuninck held JACOBSEN Fabio off UAE wheels until 1km, refusing to share pace burden despite UAE controlling.
Alpecin launched PHILIPSEN at ~300m when Groupama and UAE leadouts expired, exploiting timing gap perfectly.
Race leader TARAXAY Rein positioned correctly near front but crashed 11km out proving even textbook positioning fails on straight roads.

rider_signals.PHILIPSEN Jasper: positive: Won from perfect leadout timing, took second stage, green jersey demonstrates best form of sprint field.
rider_signals.JACOBSEN Fabio: neutral: Positioned too far back in final 500m despite Deceuninck controlling pace, tactical positioning error cost stage.
rider_signals.MOLANO Juan Sebastian: neutral: UAE burned leadout too early for him at 1.6km, fourth place shows speed but team tactical timing flawed.
rider_signals.TARAXAY Rein: negative: Lost red jersey to crash 11km out despite correct positioning, suggests handling vulnerability on fast straight roads.
rider_signals.ALISSON Kenny: neutral: Inherited red jersey via TARAXAY crash, gains 25 seconds to ROGLIC Primoz without effort.
